name: Herbed Roasted Salmon and Vegetables W/Lemon
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine white wine, fish stock, shallots, and lemon juice in a saucepan. Boil over high heat until reduced by half (to just under 1/2 cup).
2. Add cream and boil until the sauce reduces to just under 2/3 cup.
3. Turn off heat and slowly whisk in butter. Stir in chives and lemon peel.
4. Combine garlic, oil, rosemary, thyme, and parsley. Pour about 2/3 into another bowl for vegetables.
5. Rinse salmon and pat dry. Rub remaining herb marinade over the fillet and season lightly with salt and pepper if desired.
6. Lay fish on a lightly oiled rimmed baking sheet or in a shallow roasting pan (can prepare up to this point earlier).
7. Bake at 400°F until flesh in center of thickest area is no longer translucent but still moist, about 20-25 minutes for fillets 1.1/4 to 1.5 inches thick.
8. Prepare vegetables: peel carrots, cut potatoes into eighths, husk corn and cut into rounds, halve zucchini diagonally, quarter bell peppers and cut crosswise into narrow triangles.
9. Toss vegetables with reserved herb marinade in a roasting pan or baking dish.
10. Bake uncovered at 400°F until vegetables brown on bottom (about 15 minutes), then stir, cover, and continue baking until potatoes are tender (20-30 more minutes).
11. If pan gets too dark, add water or white wine to prevent burning.
12. Serve salmon with roasted vegetables and lemon chive sauce.
